issue_title: Google Assistant executes twice a switch on turn on / off (stateless switches)
issue_body:
## The problem
Hey, i have some stateless switches defined, a few of them i use for my logitech remote to send commands
for example, i say to google : 
Hey Google, channel up on
Or 
Hey Google, channel up Off

it should turn on my switch, and should send one "command up" command, but instead , it excutes twice, you can also see that in the debug log below, there is a EXECUTE and a QUERY command  1 sec later
Normal switches with state or lights, then its fine, seems only on stateless switches?

wheni turn on the switch NOT from google, but from HA itself, then its working like it should
it only happens when i trigger the command from Google Assistant

## Environment
- Home Assistant Core release with the issue: 117.1
- Last working Home Assistant Core release (if known):  not sure, it worked before, but last time i used was like 1 year ago
- Operating environment (OS/Container/Supervised/Core): HassOS
- Integration causing this issue: Google Assistant nabucasa

## Problem-relevant `configuration.yaml`
```
#google config : 

    switch.harmony_channel_up:
      name: Kanaal omhoog
      room: Harmony
      aliases:
        - Kanaal omhoog
        - Zender omhoog
        - Channel up
		
#switch config

   harmony_channel_up:
     value_template: "false"
     turn_on:
       - service: script.harmony_channel_up
     turn_off:
       - service: script.harmony_channel_down		
	   
#script config

harmony_channel_up:
  alias: Kanaal omhoog
  sequence:
  - service: remote.send_command
    data:
      entity_id: remote.harmony
      device: !secret harmony_tv
      command: ChannelUp
harmony_channel_down:
  alias: Kanaal omhoog
  sequence:
  - service: remote.send_command
    data:
      entity_id: remote.harmony
      device: !secret harmony_tv
      command: ChannelDown
```

## Traceback/Error logs


```
2020-10-31 23:02:48 DEBUG (MainThread) [hass_nabucasa.iot] Received message:
{'handler': 'google_actions',
 'msgid': '380c6965-7951-4fa9-8a21-1a25884e644c',
 'payload': {'inputs': [{'context': {'locale_country': 'NL',
                                     'locale_language': 'nl'},
                         'intent': 'action.devices.EXECUTE',
                         'payload': {'commands': [{'devices': [{'customData': {'baseUrl': 'https://xx.ui.nabu.casa',
                                                                               'httpPort': 8123,
                                                                               'httpSSL': False,
                                                                               'proxyDeviceId': '40902f43-31b8-4ccc-ae62-026ab8f3dc4d',
                                                                               'uuid': 'b436ccf322f94b788b7e094d302fbdad',
                                                                               'webhookId': '2338d36ab1868711dd75786113c6dd01b5b806a42bda73719309742a20f2fa31'},
                                                                'id': 'switch.harmony_channel_up'}],
                                                   'execution': [{'command': 'action.devices.commands.OnOff',
                                                                  'params': {'on': True}}]}]}}],
             'requestId': '17005342027216910750'}}

2020-10-31 23:02:49 DEBUG (MainThread) [hass_nabucasa.iot] Publishing message:
{'msgid': '380c6965-7951-4fa9-8a21-1a25884e644c',
 'payload': {'payload': {'commands': [{'ids': ['switch.harmony_channel_up'],
                                       'states': {'on': False, 'online': True},
                                       'status': 'SUCCESS'}]},
             'requestId': '17005342027216910750'}}

2020-10-31 23:02:50 DEBUG (MainThread) [hass_nabucasa.iot] Received message:
{'handler': 'google_actions',
 'msgid': 'b97bdcc0-9f7f-4648-8037-f1aa8e18c125',
 'payload': {'inputs': [{'intent': 'action.devices.QUERY',
                         'payload': {'devices': [{'customData': {'baseUrl': 'https://xxx.ui.nabu.casa',
                                                                 'httpPort': 8123,
                                                                 'httpSSL': False,
                                                                 'proxyDeviceId': '40902f43-31b8-4ccc-ae62-026ab8f3dc4d',
                                                                 'uuid': 'b436ccf322f94b788b7e094d302fbdad',
                                                                 'webhookId': '2338d36ab1868711dd75786113c6dd01b5b806a42bda73719309742a20f2fa31'},
                                                  'id': 'switch.harmony_channel_up'}]}}],
             'requestId': '3012232863068751350'}}

2020-10-31 23:02:50 DEBUG (MainThread) [hass_nabucasa.iot] Publishing message:
{'msgid': 'b97bdcc0-9f7f-4648-8037-f1aa8e18c125',
 'payload': {'payload': {'devices': {'switch.harmony_channel_up': {'on': False,
                                                                   'online': True}}},
             'requestId': '3012232863068751350'}}
```

pr_title: Don't wait for Google Assistant service calls when reporting state
pr_body:
<!--
  You are amazing! Thanks for contributing to our project!
  Please, DO NOT DELETE ANY TEXT from this template! (unless instructed).
-->
## Proposed change
<!--
  Describe the big picture of your changes here to communicate to the
  maintainers why we should accept this pull request. If it fixes a bug
  or resolves a feature request, be sure to link to that issue in the
  additional information section.
-->
Don't wait for Google Assistant service calls when reporting state

If not reporting state, wait only 2s for service calls to finish

This should make it less likely that Google Assistant times out or commands are executed twice

## Type of change
<!--
  What type of change does your PR introduce to Home Assistant?
  NOTE: Please, check only 1! box!
  If your PR requires multiple boxes to be checked, you'll most likely need to
  split it into multiple PRs. This makes things easier and faster to code review.
-->

- [ ] Dependency upgrade
- [x] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New integration (thank you!)
- [ ] New feature (which adds functionality to an existing integration)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix/feature causing existing functionality to break)
- [ ] Code quality improvements to existing code or addition of tests

## Additional information
<!--
  Details are important, and help maintainers processing your PR.
  Please be sure to fill out additional details, if applicable.
-->

- This PR fixes or closes issue: fixes #
- This PR is related to issue: 
- Link to documentation pull request:

If the code communicates with devices, web services, or third-party tools:

      Updated and included derived files by running: `python3 -m script.hassfest`.
- [ ] New or updated dependencies have been added to `requirements_all.txt`.

- [ ] For the updated dependencies - a link to the changelog, or at minimum a diff between library versions is added to the PR description.

The integration reached or maintains the following [Integration Quality Scale][quality-scale]:
